rulings.Case Title: VADIM RYABOY VS. COASTAL TRADE SECURITIES, LLC A DELAWARE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Mark Preston
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 6, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANT MARC PRESTON, CHRISTOPHER APOLITO, COASTAL TRADE SECURITIES, LLC, SASHA SZABO'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons. The motion to quash service is granted in part. To effectuate substitute service: a copy of the summons and complaint must be left at the individual's dwelling house ("usual place of abode") with a "competent member of the household" or person "apparently in charge of his or her office, place of business, or usual mailing address," at least 18 years old, who must be told what the papers are. [See CCP § 415.20(b).] The person with whom the papers are left need not be a member of the family. [See Bein v. Brechtel-Jochim Group, Inc. (1992) 6 CA4th 1387, 1393.] For example, a doorman in an apartment building is considered a "competent member of the household" where they control access to the residence. [Id. at 1393.] Here, Plainitff served Mr. Apolito at 32 Highland Drive, in New Jersey. This is not Defendant Apolito's residence, but is the residence of his mother. Defendant Apolito does not live at this address. Therefore the Court finds the substitute service to be invalid and grants the motion to quash as to Defendant Apolito. The proof of service filed with the court shows Defendant Szabo was filed via substitute service at his place of business at 40 Wall Street, 17th Floor, New York, New York. This is valid substitute service. Defendant argues Plaintiff served Defendant Szabo by leaving documents with his doorman, however this is also valid service under CA law. Therefore, the court denies the motion as to Defendant Szabo. The proof of service filed with the court shows Defendant Costal Trade Securities was filed via substitute service on Jessica Porter at its place of business 40 Wall Street, 17th Floor, New York, New York. This is valid substitute service. A second proof of service shows Mr. Preston was served at 425 Second Street STE 250. The court finds there has been valid service on agent for service of process Marc Preston for Costal Trade Securities, therefore the motion is denied as to Defendant Costal Trade Securities. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: MATTHEW KUMIN VS. STEPHEN SOMMERS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiff Matthew Kumins Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And For A Monetary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 6, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF MATTHEW KUMIN'S Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiff Matthew Kumins Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And For A Monetary Sanction. Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is continued to August 24, 2015, because Judge Highman recuses himself. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL F. GOODMAN MD ET AL VS. CAMICO INSURANCE SERVICES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena Seeking Production Of Business Records From Michele A. Mclaughlin, And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 6, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFFS DANIEL GOODMAN, AND DANIEL F. GOODMAN, MD, A MEDICAL CORPORATION'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena Seeking Production Of Business Records From Michele A. Mclaughlin, And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Requests 2, 3, 6-8, 10, 13, 14, 16, 17-- McLaughlin shall produce all responsive documents. Requests 5, 9, 11, 12, 15, 19-22-- Only responsive documents that relate to Goodman's 2008 or 2009 tax returns (including without limitation any audits or other government actions or malpractice claims related thereto) shall be produced. Request 1-- McLaughlin shall produce documents sufficient to show whether she has been licensed as a CPA in California at all times from January 1, 2008, through the present. Request 4-- McLaughlin shall produce all cancelled checks that are responsive to this request as she agreed to do. The court might revisit this issue if defendants claim under oath that McLaughlin didn't pay her premiums during the relevant time periods. Request 18-- McLaughlin shall produce all responsive documents not protected by attorney-client privilege or attorney work product. McLaughlin shall provide a privilege log as to all documents withheld on grounds of attorney-client privilege other than documents where the sole author is McLaughlin or Aaron & Wilson and the sole recipient is McLaughlin or Aaron & Wilson. McLaughlin shall also provide the privilege log as to all documents withheld on grounds of attorney work product other than documents written by Aaron & Wilson that were not seen by anyone other than an attorney or employee of Aaron & Wilson or a consulting expert. Request 23-- McLaughlin shall not produce any documents in response to this request. All documents ordered to be produced shall be produced within 20 days of service of the order. A protective order shall be issued as to Requests 2-22. Hearing required to discuss the protective order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SCOTT SMITH ET AL VS. WENDELL BROWN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 6, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANT WENDELL BROWN'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons. The motion to quash service is granted. To effectuate substitute service: a copy of the summons and complaint must be left at the individual's dwelling house ("usual place of abode") with a "competent member of the household" or person "apparently in charge of his or her office, place of business, or usual mailing address," at least 18 years old, who must be told what the papers are. [See CCP § 415.20(b).] The person with whom the papers are left need not be a member of the family. [See Bein v. Brechtel-Jochim Group, Inc. (1992) 6 CA4th 1387, 1393.] Here, it is undisputed that Plaintiff left the summons and complaint at 7 Dry Brook Trail in Henderson, NV. However, 7 Dry Brook Trail is not Defendant's dwelling house under CCP § 415.20(b). Further, the summons was not left with a "competent member of the household" as required for substitute service. Therefore the Court finds the substitute service to be invalid. Sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)
